SEARCH
Package net.sourceforge.plantuml.klimt.drawing.svg

Class XmlWriter

java.lang.Object
net.sourceforge.plantuml.klimt.drawing.svg.XmlWriter
public class XmlWriter extends Object
A minimal, dependency-free streaming XML/SVG writer.

Designed to run under TeaVM (no DOM, no javax.xml). It keeps a stack of currently open element names so it can auto-close them and produce self-closing tags (<g/>) when an element has no children.

The API is fluent: calls can be chained. Element text and attribute values are XML-escaped, with the minimal escaping required for each context (text content only needs & and <; attribute values additionally need ").

Beyond elements, attributes and text, it can also emit comments, processing instructions, CDATA sections and raw (already-serialized) markup. Raw markup is the caller's responsibility to escape; this writer passes it through verbatim.

  • Constructor Details

    • XmlWriter

      public XmlWriter(int indentSpaces)

  • Method Details

    • startElement

      public XmlWriter startElement(String name)

    • attribute

      public XmlWriter attribute(String name, String value)

    • text

      public XmlWriter text(String value)

    • comment

      public XmlWriter comment(String value)
      Emits an XML comment <!--...--> with no decorative spacing, matching the legacy serializer. The sequence -- (illegal inside a comment) is defanged to - -; a trailing - would otherwise merge with the closing -->, so a single space is inserted in that case only.

    • processingInstruction

      public XmlWriter processingInstruction(String target, String data)
      Emits a processing instruction <?target data?>, e.g. <?plantuml version?>.

    • cdata

      public XmlWriter cdata(String value)
      Emits a CDATA section <![CDATA[ ... ]]>, used for embedded CSS and scripts. The content is not escaped; the only illegal sequence ]]> is split across two sections to keep the output well-formed.

    • raw

      public XmlWriter raw(String markup)
      Emits already-serialized markup verbatim, with no escaping and no indentation. Intended for splicing in pre-built SVG fragments (e.g. inlined images). The caller is responsible for the well-formedness of markup.

    • endElement

      public XmlWriter endElement()

    • getXml

      public String getXml()

    • getRawXml

      public String getRawXml()
      Returns the markup produced so far, without requiring that every element be closed. Use this to retrieve a fragment that will be spliced into another writer via raw(String); use getXml() for a complete document, where leaving an element open is a programming error.
